report_metadata.cancer_type: Floor of Mouth Squamous Cell Carcinoma
report_metadata.patient_context: 45-year-old male, heavy smoker and heavy alcohol use, hepatic steatosis (fatty liver disease)
special_considerations: Mandible periosteal invasion requires marginal vs segmental mandibulectomy decision
Free flap reconstruction required if segmental mandibulectomy performed
PD-L1 CPS 15 qualifies for perioperative pembrolizumab (KEYNOTE-689)
Liver steatosis may affect 5-FU and methotrexate metabolism but cisplatin is renally cleared
Tobacco and alcohol cessation is critical — 3.7x higher odds of complete response in quitters

treatments.name: Perioperative Pembrolizumab + Surgery + Adjuvant CRT (KEYNOTE-689 Regimen)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCT (KEYNOTE-689, N=714) published in NEJM 2025; FDA approved June 2025 for PD-L1 CPS>=1 LA-HNSCC

survival_benefit.rationale: EFS HR 0.70 (CPS>=1); median EFS 59.7 vs 29.6 months; 36-month EFS 58.2% vs 44.9%; DMFS HR 0.71. First positive perioperative immunotherapy trial in HNSCC in >20 years

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved June 13, 2025 for PD-L1 CPS>=1 resectable LA-HNSCC; patient has CPS 15 qualifying him for this regimen

safety_profile.rationale: Grade 3+ AEs 44.6% vs 42.9% control; stomatitis 48%, radiation skin injury 40%, weight loss 36%; immune-mediated hepatitis 0.6% — liver steatosis requires monitoring for hepatic irAEs

biomarker_match.rationale: PD-L1 CPS 15 exceeds the CPS>=1 threshold; CPS>=10 subgroup showed even stronger benefit (HR 0.66); patient is an ideal candidate

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pembrolizumab is an anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody that blocks the PD-1/PD-L1 checkpoint interaction, restoring T-cell-mediated antitumor immunity. Administered as 2 neoadjuvant cycles before surgery, then 15 adjuvant cycles with radiation +/- cisplatin. Neoadjuvant pembrolizumab primes systemic immune response while tumor is in situ.
notable_side_effects: Immune-mediated hepatitis (0.6%) — requires close monitoring with hepatic steatosis

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab Monotherapy (Recurrent/Metastatic Setting — KEYNOTE-048)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCT (KEYNOTE-048); FDA approved first-line for R/M HNSCC with PD-L1 CPS>=1; established standard for recurrent disease

survival_benefit.rationale: Significantly improved OS vs EXTREME in CPS>=20 and CPS>=1 populations; provides treatment option if disease recurs after primary surgery/CRT; reserved for recurrence scenario

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved and widely available; CPS 15 qualifies the patient

safety_profile.rationale: Well-tolerated monotherapy; fewer toxicities than chemotherapy combinations; immune-mediated hepatitis risk requires monitoring with hepatic steatosis

biomarker_match.rationale: PD-L1 CPS 15 places patient in favorable subgroup; higher CPS correlated with better outcomes

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Anti-PD-1 checkpoint inhibitor that blocks PD-1/PD-L1 interaction to restore endogenous antitumor immune response. Administered as 200mg IV q3 weeks as monotherapy for PD-L1+ R/M HNSCC. Provides durable responses in a subset of patients with favorable PD-L1 expression.

treatments.name: Primary Surgery (Marginal Mandibulectomy) + Adjuvant Cisplatin-Based Chemoradiation

evidence_level.rationale: NCCN Category 1 recommendation; supported by multiple Phase 3 RCTs (RTOG 9501, EORTC 22931) establishing adjuvant CRT as standard for high-risk resected HNSCC

survival_benefit.rationale: 3-year OS ~78% with cisplatin-CRT for oral cavity SCC with high-risk features; 5-year OS ~56%; LRC rates 85% with cisplatin vs 62% with alternatives. Marginal mandibulectomy appropriate for periosteal-only invasion with comparable local control (RR=0.98, p=0.58)

accessibility.rationale: Universally available standard of care at all cancer centers; cisplatin 100mg/m2 q3w is established protocol

safety_profile.rationale: Grade 3+ AEs 77% with CRT; cisplatin nephrotoxicity and ototoxicity significant; mucositis severe in oral cavity. Cisplatin is renally excreted so hepatic steatosis does not require dose reduction, but 5-FU if used requires hepatic monitoring

biomarker_match.rationale: Universal applicability; no biomarker requirements for standard CRT

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Composite curative-intent approach: wide local excision of floor of mouth tumor with marginal mandibulectomy (preserving mandibular continuity when periosteal invasion is confirmed without cortical/medullary involvement), ipsilateral selective neck dissection (levels I-IV) for N1 disease, followed by adjuvant radiation (60-66 Gy) with concurrent high-dose cisplatin (100 mg/m2 q3 weeks x3 cycles). Marginal mandibulectomy preserves mandibular height and avoids need for free flap reconstruction.

key_evidence.study_name: RTOG 9501 / EORTC 22931 / Marginal vs Segmental Mandibulectomy Meta-analysis
key_evidence.journal: Journal of Clinical Oncology / International Archives of Otorhinolaryngology

treatments.name: Segmental Mandibulectomy + Fibula Free Flap Reconstruction + Adjuvant CRT

evidence_level.rationale: Systematic review/meta-analysis of 2,023 patients; NCCN recommended for medullary or extensive cortical invasion; fibula free flap is gold standard with 99% flap survival rate

survival_benefit.rationale: Segmental mandibulectomy shows 73% improved DFS over marginal when medullary invasion present (HR 0.27, p=0.04). Critical if intraoperative pathology reveals cortical invasion beyond periosteum. 5-year DFS significantly superior with SM for medullary invasion

accessibility.rationale: Available at major head and neck cancer centers with microvascular surgery capability; requires experienced microvascular team for fibula free flap

safety_profile.rationale: Major surgery with 8-12 hour OR time; donor site morbidity; 41.5% early complication rate (higher in irradiated fields); requires dental rehabilitation with implants (95% implant survival)

biomarker_match.rationale: Universal applicability based on anatomic extent of invasion; critical surgical decision based on intraoperative frozen section pathology

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Full-thickness resection of mandibular segment when periosteal invasion extends to cortical or medullary bone. The vascularized fibula free flap provides composite bone and soft tissue reconstruction maintaining mandibular continuity. The fibula's bicortical structure allows dental implant placement for oral rehabilitation. Flap is anastomosed to recipient vessels (typically facial artery/vein). Adjuvant CRT follows after wound healing (typically 6 weeks post-op).

key_evidence.study_name: Marginal vs Segmental Mandibulectomy Meta-analysis (Alves et al.)

biomarker_requirements: Cortical or medullary mandibular invasion on imaging or intraoperative frozen section
notable_side_effects: Donor site morbidity (ankle stiffness, gait changes)
Free flap failure (<1% in experienced centers)
Prolonged recovery (3-6 months before adjuvant therapy possible)
Late complications in 38.2% (higher in irradiated fields)
treatments.availability: Available at major academic head and neck cancer centers

treatments.name: Nivolumab (Second-Line, Recurrent/Metastatic — CheckMate 141)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCT (CheckMate 141, N=361); FDA approved for platinum-refractory R/M HNSCC; NEJM published landmark study

survival_benefit.rationale: Median OS 7.5 vs 5.1 months (HR 0.70, p=0.01); 1-year OS 36% vs 16.6%; modest absolute benefit but important as second-line salvage option

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved; widely available for platinum-refractory setting; no biomarker requirement for eligibility

safety_profile.rationale: Well-tolerated vs standard chemotherapy; grade 3-4 AEs 13% vs 35%; hepatic irAEs possible but rare

biomarker_match.rationale: Approved regardless of PD-L1 status; however, PD-L1 CPS 15 may predict better response; TP53 mutation effect on immunotherapy response remains under investigation

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody that blocks PD-1 receptor on T cells, preventing tumor-mediated immune suppression. Administered 240mg IV q2 weeks or 480mg q4 weeks. Reserved for platinum-refractory recurrent/metastatic disease as salvage therapy.

treatments.name: Cetuximab + Radiation (Cisplatin-Unfit Alternative)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 Bonner trial established cetuximab-RT; multiple Phase 3 trials (ARTSCAN III) comparing to cisplatin-RT; NCCN listed alternative for cisplatin-unfit patients

survival_benefit.rationale: Inferior to cisplatin-RT in ARTSCAN III (OS and LRC significantly worse); however, provides meaningful benefit over RT alone. 3-year OS approximately 55% in LA-HNSCC. Reserved if liver steatosis progresses to impair renal function or patient becomes cisplatin-ineligible

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved and widely available; cetuximab 400mg/m2 loading then 250mg/m2 weekly during RT

safety_profile.rationale: Better tolerated than cisplatin-RT; acneiform rash (common, correlates with response); fewer treatment breaks; less nephrotoxicity; no hepatotoxicity concerns

biomarker_match.rationale: EGFR is universally overexpressed in HNSCC (>90%); no specific biomarker testing required; however, benefit less established in oral cavity vs oropharynx

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Cetuximab is a chimeric anti-EGFR monoclonal antibody that blocks EGF receptor signaling, inhibiting tumor proliferation, angiogenesis, and metastasis while enhancing radiosensitivity. Administered weekly concurrent with definitive or adjuvant radiation therapy. Alternative for patients who cannot tolerate cisplatin.

treatments.name: TPF Induction Chemotherapy (Docetaxel/Cisplatin/5-FU) Followed by Surgery

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 data available but mixed results; TPF induction failed to improve OS in unselected OSCC population; however, patients with favorable pathological response show improved outcomes

survival_benefit.rationale: 27% reduction in death risk vs PF alone (mOS 18.8 vs 14.5 months); pCR 13.4%; favorable pathological response rate 27.7% — responders derive meaningful benefit but overall population benefit uncertain

accessibility.rationale: Widely available regimen; all components FDA approved; standard neoadjuvant option at many centers

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity: febrile neutropenia, mucositis, diarrhea. 5-FU undergoes hepatic metabolism — liver steatosis increases risk of 5-FU toxicity (DPD pathway). Cisplatin nephrotoxicity. Heavy alcohol history may compound hepatotoxicity risk

biomarker_match.rationale: Universal applicability; no biomarker requirement. TP53 mutation may predict chemoresistance but data inconclusive for TPF

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Triple-drug induction: Docetaxel (75 mg/m2 D1) stabilizes microtubules causing mitotic arrest; Cisplatin (75 mg/m2 D1) forms DNA crosslinks; 5-Fluorouracil (750 mg/m2/day D1-5) inhibits thymidylate synthase disrupting DNA synthesis. Given for 2-3 cycles before definitive surgery. Goal is tumor downstaging and treatment of micrometastatic disease. CAUTION: 5-FU hepatic metabolism impaired by steatosis — dose adjustment may be needed.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase III TPF Induction in Oral Cavity SCC (Licitra et al.)
key_evidence.journal: Journal of Clinical Oncology / Cancer Science

treatments.name: Petosemtamab (MCLA-158) + Pembrolizumab

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 data showing ORR 60-67% in combination with pembrolizumab; FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation 2024; Phase 3 LiGeR-HN1 and LiGeR-HN2 trials ongoing with interim data expected 2026

survival_benefit.rationale: Monotherapy ORR 40.4%, mPFS 5.1 months; combination with pembrolizumab ORR 60-67%; responses appear durable; substantial improvement over historical controls

accessibility.rationale: Available only through Phase 3 clinical trials (LiGeR-HN1: NCT06525220; LiGeR-HN2: NCT06496178); FDA Breakthrough designation may accelerate approval

safety_profile.rationale: Well-tolerated in combination; manageable adverse event profile in Phase 2; no specific hepatic concerns reported

biomarker_match.rationale: EGFR universally overexpressed in HNSCC; LGR5 cancer stem cell marker broadly expressed; PD-L1 CPS 15 qualifies for pembrolizumab component

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Petosemtamab is a bispecific antibody targeting both EGFR and LGR5 (a cancer stem cell marker) with ADCC-enhanced activity. It triggers EGFR degradation specifically on LGR5+ cancer stem cells, depleting the treatment-resistant stem cell population while sparing normal EGFR+ epithelial cells. Combined with pembrolizumab for dual immune/targeted attack.
biomarker_requirements: PD-L1 CPS >= 1 for combination with pembrolizumab

treatments.name: Palbociclib + Cetuximab (CDK4/6 Inhibition — CDKN2A-altered HNSCC)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 data showing ORR 39% in platinum-resistant HNSCC; Phase 3 trial ongoing (NCT04966481) in CDKN2A-altered disease; TP53-mutant tumors often have concurrent cell cycle pathway alterations

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 39% in platinum-resistant patients; 19% in cetuximab-resistant; activity in heavily pretreated population is encouraging but survival data immature

accessibility.rationale: Palbociclib FDA approved for breast cancer; Phase 3 trial ongoing for HNSCC (NCT04966481); accessible off-label if CDKN2A alteration confirmed

safety_profile.rationale: Neutropenia is primary toxicity (manageable with dose modification); oral administration convenient; no significant hepatotoxicity

biomarker_match.rationale: Requires CDKN2A alteration for optimal benefit; TP53 mutation often co-occurs with cell cycle pathway dysregulation; would need genomic profiling to confirm CDKN2A status

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Palbociclib inhibits CDK4/6 kinases that drive G1-to-S cell cycle transition. Combined with cetuximab (anti-EGFR), this addresses both cell cycle deregulation (common in TP53-mutant, CDKN2A-altered HNSCC) and EGFR-driven proliferation. CDK4/6 inhibition restores Rb-mediated cell cycle control and may synergize with EGFR blockade.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2 Palbociclib + Cetuximab in HPV-negative HNSCC

treatments.name: Ficerafusp (BCA101) + Pembrolizumab (Anti-EGFR/TGF-beta Bifunctional)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2/3 FORTIFI-HN01 trial ongoing; early Phase 1/2 data showing ORR 54% overall and 64% in HPV-negative patients (relevant to this patient)

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 54% overall, 64% in HPV-negative cohort; encouraging activity exceeding historical controls for combination regimens; survival data maturing

accessibility.rationale: Available only through FORTIFI-HN01 Phase 2/3 clinical trial; not yet approved

safety_profile.rationale: Manageable toxicity profile in early studies; skin toxicity expected from EGFR component; TGF-beta trap may have unique irAE profile

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets EGFR (ubiquitous in HNSCC) and TGF-beta (immunosuppressive pathway); HPV-negative status (typical for oral cavity/smoking-related) may predict better response

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Ficerafusp is a bifunctional fusion protein combining an anti-EGFR antibody with a TGF-beta receptor extracellular domain (ligand trap). This dual mechanism simultaneously blocks EGFR-driven tumor proliferation and neutralizes TGF-beta-mediated immune suppression in the tumor microenvironment. Combined with pembrolizumab for enhanced antitumor immunity.

treatments.name: Alpelisib (PIK3CA-Targeted Therapy — TRIUMPH Trial)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 umbrella trial (KCSG HN 15-16 TRIUMPH); 42 patients treated with alpelisib; response evaluation in 33 patients; differential efficacy by mutation site published 2025

survival_benefit.rationale: Helical domain mutations (E542K, E545K) showed mPFS 7.3 months; kinase domain H1047R showed poorer mPFS 1.6 months. Benefit depends on specific PIK3CA mutation site. Patient needs mutation characterization

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for PIK3CA-mutant HR+/HER2- breast cancer but off-label for HNSCC; availability through clinical trials or compassionate use

safety_profile.rationale: Hyperglycemia (major concern — 64% all grades); rash; diarrhea; hepatic steatosis may exacerbate metabolic effects; well-tolerated overall in TRIUMPH but metabolic monitoring essential

biomarker_match.rationale: Patient has confirmed PIK3CA mutation — direct biomarker match. Specific mutation site (helical vs kinase domain) determines expected efficacy. TP53 co-mutation did not notably influence alpelisib response

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Alpelisib is a selective PI3Kalpha inhibitor that blocks PIK3CA-driven oncogenic signaling through the PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway. PIK3CA mutations occur in ~14% of OSCC, driving tumor proliferation and treatment resistance. Targets the specific molecular alteration present in this patient's tumor. Efficacy depends on the specific PIK3CA mutation site.

treatments.name: Enfortumab Vedotin (Anti-Nectin-4 ADC)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 EV-202 trial in R/M HNSCC (N=46); demonstrates proof-of-concept activity; Phase 3 trials justified but not yet completed

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 23.9%, DCR 56.5%, mPFS 3.9 months; modest single-agent activity in heavily pretreated patients; potential greater benefit in combination with IO

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for urothelial carcinoma; off-label or clinical trial access for HNSCC; combination trials with pembrolizumab ongoing

safety_profile.rationale: Alopecia, fatigue, peripheral neuropathy are common; skin reactions; manageable toxicity profile; no specific hepatic metabolism concerns

biomarker_match.rationale: Nectin-4 is expressed in majority of HNSCC tumors; no companion diagnostic required; broad applicability

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Enfortumab vedotin is an antibody-drug conjugate targeting Nectin-4, which is highly expressed on HNSCC cells. The anti-Nectin-4 antibody delivers the cytotoxic payload MMAE (monomethyl auristatin E) directly to tumor cells, causing microtubule disruption and apoptosis while minimizing systemic toxicity.

treatments.name: Neoadjuvant Nivolumab + Ipilimumab (Dual Checkpoint — Oral Cavity)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 neoadjuvant trial (Schoenfeld et al., N=29 oral cavity patients); CheckMate 714/651 Phase 2/3 in R/M setting showed limited benefit overall but signal in high CPS

survival_benefit.rationale: Neoadjuvant MPR 20% with combination vs 7% with nivo alone in oral cavity; CheckMate 651 showed OS trend in CPS>=20 but missed primary endpoint in total population

accessibility.rationale: Both drugs FDA approved individually; combination use in HNSCC remains off-label/investigational

safety_profile.rationale: Dual checkpoint blockade increases irAE risk: hepatitis, colitis, pneumonitis; immune-mediated hepatitis particularly concerning with hepatic steatosis; grade 3+ AEs higher than monotherapy

biomarker_match.rationale: PD-L1 CPS 15 suggests immunologically active tumor; dual checkpoint may overcome resistance; TP53 mutation implications for neoantigen load remain investigational

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Dual immune checkpoint blockade: Nivolumab (anti-PD-1) releases the PD-1/PD-L1 brake on T cell activation while Ipilimumab (anti-CTLA-4) enhances T cell priming and activation in lymph nodes. The combination generates a more robust and diverse antitumor immune response than either agent alone, particularly relevant in tumors with moderate PD-L1 expression.

key_evidence.study_name: Schoenfeld et al. Neoadjuvant Nivo/Ipi in Oral Cavity SCC / CheckMate 651

notable_side_effects: Immune-mediated hepatitis (5-10%) — HIGH RISK with hepatic steatosis

treatments.name: Tisotumab Vedotin (Anti-Tissue Factor ADC)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 InnovaTV 207 trial in HNSCC showing ORR 32.5%; FDA approved for cervical cancer; HNSCC data emerging

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 32.5%, median DOR 5.6 months in HNSCC; moderate activity as single agent in pretreated patients; combination strategies may enhance benefit

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for cervical cancer; HNSCC use requires clinical trial enrollment or off-label access

safety_profile.rationale: Ocular toxicity (conjunctivitis, corneal adverse events) unique to this ADC; bleeding events; peripheral neuropathy; requires ophthalmologic monitoring

biomarker_match.rationale: Tissue factor broadly expressed in HNSCC; no companion diagnostic required; universal target expression

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Tisotumab vedotin is an antibody-drug conjugate targeting tissue factor (TF/CD142), which is overexpressed on tumor cells. The anti-TF antibody delivers the MMAE cytotoxic payload to tumor cells, causing microtubule disruption and cell death. TF is involved in tumor angiogenesis and metastasis, providing an additional mechanism of action.

treatments.name: Ficlatuzumab (Anti-HGF) + Cetuximab (MET Pathway Inhibition)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 data in HPV-negative HNSCC showing ORR 38%; Phase 3 FIERCE-HN trial enrolling (NCT06064877)

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 38%, mPFS 4.1 months in HPV-negative cohort; oral cavity cancers are predominantly HPV-negative making this a relevant target

accessibility.rationale: Available only through FIERCE-HN Phase 3 clinical trial; not yet approved for any indication

safety_profile.rationale: Generally well-tolerated; lower toxicity than cytotoxic chemotherapy; standard anti-EGFR side effects from cetuximab component

biomarker_match.rationale: HPV-negative oral cavity SCC is target population; HGF/c-MET pathway activation as resistance mechanism; no companion diagnostic established

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Ficlatuzumab is a humanized monoclonal antibody that blocks hepatocyte growth factor (HGF), preventing activation of the c-MET receptor tyrosine kinase. c-MET signaling drives tumor invasion, metastasis, and resistance to EGFR inhibitors. Combined with cetuximab to simultaneously block EGFR and MET pathways, addressing a common resistance mechanism in oral cavity SCC.

clinical_trials.title: KEYNOTE-689: Perioperative Pembrolizumab in Resectable LA-HNSCC
clinical_trials.early_results: EFS HR 0.70 (CPS>=1, p=0.0014); HR 0.66 (CPS>=10, p=0.0022); median EFS 59.7 vs 29.6 months. Now standard of care. Patient with CPS 15 is eligible and recommended for this regimen.

clinical_trials.title: LiGeR-HN1: Petosemtamab (MCLA-158) + Pembrolizumab vs Pembrolizumab in 1L R/M PD-L1+ HNSCC
clinical_trials.early_results: Phase 2 data: ORR 60-67% with combination vs historical ~23% with pembro alone; FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation granted 2024. Interim Phase 3 data expected 2026.

clinical_trials.title: LiGeR-HN2: Petosemtamab Monotherapy vs Standard of Care in 2L+ R/M HNSCC
clinical_trials.early_results: Phase 2 monotherapy ORR 40.4%, mPFS 5.1 months; encouraging durability of responses. No biomarker selection required.

clinical_trials.title: Palbociclib + Cetuximab in CDKN2A-altered HPV-negative R/M HNSCC
clinical_trials.early_results: Phase 2: ORR 39% in platinum-resistant; 19% in cetuximab-resistant; CDK4/6 inhibition synergizes with EGFR blockade in cell cycle-deregulated HNSCC (common with TP53 mutation).

clinical_trials.title: FIERCE-HN: Ficlatuzumab + Cetuximab in HPV-negative R/M HNSCC
clinical_trials.early_results: Phase 2: ORR 38%, mPFS 4.1 months in HPV-negative cohort; c-MET pathway inhibition addresses cetuximab resistance mechanism common in oral cavity SCC.

clinical_trials.title: MCLA-129 (EGFR x c-MET Bispecific) in Advanced Solid Tumors Including HNSCC
clinical_trials.early_results: 17% partial response rate, 67% disease control rate in evaluable HNSCC patients; dual EGFR/MET targeting with enhanced ADCC/ADCP.

clinical_trials.title: HERTHENA-PanTumor01: Patritumab Deruxtecan (HER3-DXd) in Solid Tumors
clinical_trials.early_results: Pan-tumor HER3-directed ADC with topoisomerase I inhibitor payload; HNSCC cohort enrolling; HER3 is expressed in ~70% of HNSCC.

combination_strategies.rationale: KEYNOTE-689 demonstrated EFS HR 0.70 when adding perioperative pembrolizumab to standard surgery + adjuvant CRT. For this patient with PD-L1 CPS 15, neoadjuvant pembro primes systemic immunity while tumor is in situ, followed by surgery with marginal mandibulectomy (periosteal invasion only), then adjuvant RT + cisplatin + pembrolizumab. This is the recommended first-line approach.

TP53-mutant oral cavity SCC frequently has concurrent CDKN2A alteration and cell cycle deregulation. CDK4/6 inhibition with palbociclib restores cell cycle control while cetuximab blocks EGFR-driven proliferation. ORR 39% in platinum-resistant setting suggests meaningful synergy. Genomic profiling for CDKN2A status recommended.

Petosemtamab targets cancer stem cells via LGR5 while blocking EGFR, combined with pembrolizumab-mediated immune activation. ORR 60-67% in combination vs ~23% pembro alone. PD-L1 CPS 15 qualifies. Addresses treatment resistance through stem cell depletion — particularly relevant for aggressive HPV-negative oral cavity SCC.

PIK3CA mutation drives PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway activation providing a direct therapeutic target. Alpelisib blocks this pathway while cetuximab targets EGFR or pembrolizumab enhances immune response. Helical domain mutations (E542K, E545K) show mPFS 7.3 months. Co-occurring TP53 mutation did not negatively impact alpelisib response in TRIUMPH data.

Tobacco cessation before treatment initiation yields 3.7x higher odds of complete response and 67% reduction in all-cause mortality (HR 0.33). Continued smoking reduces chemotherapy/RT efficacy, increases mucositis severity, impairs wound healing (critical for mandibulectomy and free flap), and elevates second primary cancer risk. Alcohol cessation reduces hepatic steatosis progression, improves cisplatin tolerance, and eliminates alcohol-mediated carcinogenesis. This is the highest-impact modifiable intervention.

supportive_care.approach: Intensive Tobacco and Alcohol Cessation Program
supportive_care.evidence: Prospective cohort study (N=134 smokers at HNSCC diagnosis): quitters had 3.7x higher odds of complete response (OR 3.7, 95% CI not reported) and 67% lower all-cause mortality (aHR 0.33, 95% CI 0.15-0.71, p=0.004). Alcohol cessation reduces hepatic steatosis progression and second primary cancer risk.
supportive_care.benefit: 67% reduction in all-cause mortality; 3.7x higher complete response rate; reduced surgical complications (wound healing, flap failure); reduced second primary cancer risk; improved hepatic steatosis (critical for chemotherapy metabolism)

supportive_care.approach: Hepatology Consultation and Liver Steatosis Management
supportive_care.evidence: Cisplatin is renally excreted and does not require hepatic dose adjustment per NCCN guidelines. However, 5-FU undergoes hepatic metabolism via DPD pathway — liver steatosis may increase 5-FU toxicity. Steatosis can progress to steatohepatitis under chemotherapy in up to 85% of patients. Immune-mediated hepatitis from pembrolizumab (0.6% in KEYNOTE-689) requires baseline hepatic function monitoring.
supportive_care.benefit: Safe chemotherapy dosing; monitoring for drug-induced liver injury; prevention of steatosis progression to steatohepatitis/cirrhosis during treatment; baseline liver function for pembrolizumab hepatic irAE monitoring

supportive_care.approach: Speech and Swallowing Rehabilitation (Pre-habilitation and Post-surgical)
supportive_care.evidence: Floor of mouth resection with mandibulectomy significantly impacts speech, swallowing, and oral competence. Fibula free flap patients report 64% functional restoration long-term. Pre-surgical baseline assessment and early post-operative rehabilitation improves functional outcomes. Dental implant rehabilitation (92-95% survival rate) enables oral intake.
supportive_care.benefit: Improved functional recovery; reduced aspiration risk; better nutritional status supporting adjuvant treatment tolerance; quality of life preservation

supportive_care.approach: Nutritional Support and PEG Tube Planning
supportive_care.evidence: Concurrent chemoradiation for oral cavity cancer causes grade 3-4 mucositis in >50% of patients. Weight loss (reported in 36% of KEYNOTE-689 patients) and dysphagia (29%) are common. Prophylactic PEG tube placement before chemoradiation recommended for T3/T4 oral cavity tumors per ESPEN guidelines. Early dietitian involvement improves treatment completion rates.
supportive_care.benefit: Maintained nutritional status during treatment; improved chemoradiation completion rates; reduced treatment breaks; supports hepatic steatosis management through dietary optimization

supportive_care.evidence: Temel et al. (NEJM 2010) demonstrated that early palliative care integration improves median OS by 2.7 months (11.6 vs 8.9 months, p=0.02) in advanced cancer. Head-and-neck-specific palliative care addresses pain management, mucositis, xerostomia, and psychosocial distress. Particularly important given heavy smoking/alcohol history and associated psychosocial comorbidities.
supportive_care.benefit: Improved quality of life; potential survival benefit; better symptom management during aggressive multimodal treatment; psychosocial support for substance cessation

supportive_care.evidence: Pre-radiation dental extraction of compromised teeth in the radiation field is standard of care to prevent osteoradionecrosis (ORN). ORN risk is 5-15% after mandibular radiation. Heavy alcohol and tobacco use increase ORN risk. Fluoride trays and saliva substitutes for xerostomia management are essential post-RT.
supportive_care.benefit: Prevention of osteoradionecrosis (a devastating complication); preservation of remaining dentition; reduced need for post-RT extractions which carry high ORN risk
